title: Entanglement Paradox
definition: The paradoxical nature of quantum entanglement, where particles become correlated and can instantaneously affect each other regardless of distance.

difficulty_levels.beginner: In the strange world of quantum mechanics, entangled particles can instantly influence each other, no matter how far apart they are.
difficulty_levels.intermediate: Quantum entanglement is a phenomenon in which two or more particles become correlated such that the state of one instantaneously affects the state of the other, regardless of distance.
difficulty_levels.expert: Entangled particles share an intrinsic correlation, making their quantum states interdependent even when separated by vast distances. This seemingly instantaneous communication is not a means for transmitting information but rather a consequence of the non-locality inherent in quantum mechanics.

extra_fields.insight: The paradoxical phenomenon of entanglement that challenges our understanding of space and time.
